headline: Malaysia Passes Comprehensive Online Safety Act 2026
context: Parliament passed landmark Online Safety Act consolidating 8 existing laws into single framework for digital regulation.

cards.big: Online Safety Act consolidates 8 laws into single digital regulatory framework
cards.sub: Act creates Digital Safety Commissioner with RM180 million annual budget

cards.big: Platforms must respond to harmful content reports within 24 hours
cards.sub: Repeated failure to act results in revenue-based fines up to 5% of turnover

cards.big: Act includes right of appeal against content removal decisions
cards.sub: Independent tribunal established to review disputed takedown orders

cards.big: Digital rights groups praised appeal mechanism but criticized broad definitions
cards.sub: Commissioner appointment by Home Minister rather than Parliament raised concerns

cards.big: Online Safety Act tests whether comprehensive regulation can balance protection with fundamental freedoms
Comprehensive Online Safety Act 2026. Good regulation balances safety enforcement with expression rights through judicial review.
